title: Script R2: Whole Metagenome Contig and Sequencing Statistics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ddkg","text":"This protocol outlines the analysis used to plot contig coverage stats, as well as sequence count and length stats. We begin with visualizing contig length vs coverage. We then visualize the distributions of sequence counts per sample as a probability density plot (similar idea as a histogram), and then do the same for median sequence length. Based on the methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2b6t9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1u6og","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"47ekc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e1d9m","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2sc03","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2a7d0","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9tuo7","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2peoi","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cntce","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4lp60","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"gkii","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3gtvu","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load the required R packages. library( library("plyr") packageVersion("plyr") library("ggplot2") packageVersion("ggplot2") library("hexbin") packageVersion("hexbin") library("reshape2") packageVersion("reshape2") ## [1] '1.8.2' ## [1] '1.0.1' ## [1] '1.27.0' ## [1] '1.4.1'

Import the tab delimited file containing the virome contig statistics. contig_stats contig_stats <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/contig_length_with_coverage_for_graphing.tsv", header=TRUE, sep='\t') head(contig_stats) %s %s ## contig count contig_length ## 1 144983_ 34 750 ## 2 603659_ 30 806 ## 3 502067_ 11 524 ## 4 105059_ 24 1091 ## 5 454425_ 53 1110 ## 6 140762_ 24 927

Generate a scatter plot comparing the number of mapped sequences to each contig, compared to the contig length. ggplot(contig_stats, aes(x=contig_length, y=count)) + theme_bw() + stat_binhex(bins=100)+ scale_fill_gradientn(colours=c(

Next we will estimate contig coverage, using a sequence length value of 150 bp. contig_stats$coverage contig_stats$coverage <- (contig_stats$count * 150) / contig_stats$contig_length head(contig_stats) %s %s ## contig count contig_length coverage ## 1 144983_ 34 750 6.800000 ## 2 603659_ 30 806 5.583127 ## 3 502067_ 11 524 3.148855 ## 4 105059_ 24 1091 3.299725 ## 5 454425_ 53 1110 7.162162 ## 6 140762_ 24 927 3.883495
Like above, we will plot the contig coverage against the contig length. ggplot(contig_stats, aes(x=contig_length, y=coverage)) + theme_bw() + stat_binhex(bins=100)+ scale_fill_gradientn(colours=c(

Next we can calculate the sequence statistics. First import the sequence summary statistics table. Be sure to change the column names of the input data frames, and to merge the data frames together for downstream processing. Here we will also import and format the mapping file. INPUT_RAW INPUT_RAW <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/raw_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_RAW) <- c("SampleID","Raw") INPUT_TRIM <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/trimmed_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_TRIM) <- c("SampleID","Trim") INPUT_HUMAN <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/human_deconseq_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_HUMAN) <- c("SampleID","Human") INPUT_NEG <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/phix_clean_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_NEG) <- c("SampleID","Neg") INPUT_MERGE <- merge(merge(merge(INPUT_RAW, INPUT_TRIM, by="SampleID"), INPUT_HUMAN, by="SampleID"), INPUT_NEG, by="SampleID", all=TRUE) INPUT_MAP <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mapping_files/SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv", header=TRUE)

We can now calculate the overall sequence statistics and plot the sequence count information after some more formatting and merging. MERGE_SUBSET MERGE_SUBSET <- MERGE_MAP[,c(2:5,1,10,11)] MERGE_SUB_RAW <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Raw","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_RAW) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_RAW$GROUP <- "Raw" MERGE_SUB_TRIM <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Trim","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_TRIM) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_TRIM$GROUP <- "Trim" MERGE_SUB_HUMAN <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Human","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_HUMAN) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_HUMAN$GROUP <- "Human" MERGE_SUB_NEG <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Neg","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_NEG) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_NEG$GROUP <- "Neg" CAT_MERGE <- rbind(MERGE_SUB_RAW, MERGE_SUB_TRIM, MERGE_SUB_HUMAN, MERGE_SUB_NEG)
Now plot the sequence count information. ggplot(CAT_MERGE_SUB, aes(x=Count, fill=GROUP)) + theme_bw() + geom_density(alpha=0.2) + scale_x_log10() + facet_wrap(~Site_Symbol, scale=

Now we are going to do the same thing as the above sequence count stats, except now we are using the sequence length stats. LENGTH_RAW LENGTH_RAW <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/raw_median_seq_length_formatted.txt", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_RAW) <- c("SampleID","Raw") LENGTH_TRIM <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/trimmed_median_seq_length_formatted.txt", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_TRIM) <- c("SampleID","Trim") LENGTH_HUMAN <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/human_cleaned_median_seq_length_formatted.txt", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_HUMAN) <- c("SampleID","Human") LENGTH_NEG <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/clean_phix_median_seq_length_formatted.txt", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_NEG) <- c("SampleID","Neg") LENGTH_MERGE <- merge(merge(merge(LENGTH_RAW, LENGTH_TRIM, by="SampleID"), LENGTH_HUMAN, by="SampleID"), LENGTH_NEG, by="SampleID", all=TRUE) MERGE_MAP <- merge(LENGTH_MERGE, INPUT_MAP, by.x="SampleID", by.y="NexteraXT_SampleID")
Plot the sequence count information. ggplot(CAT_MERGE_SUB, aes(x=Count, fill=GROUP)) + theme_bw() + geom_density(alpha=0.2) + facet_wrap(~Site_Symbol, scale=

Check the head of this data frame. head(CAT_MERGE_SUB) %s %s ## Count SampleID Site_Symbol GROUP ## 1 676336 MG100128 Ra Raw ## 2 675067 MG100129 Ra Raw ## 3 584346 MG100130 Ra Raw ## 4 585573 MG100131 Ra Raw ## 5 662992 MG100132 Ra Raw ## 6 730349 MG100133 Ra Raw

Prepare the format to plot sequence length. MERGE_SUBSET MERGE_SUBSET <- MERGE_MAP[,c(2:5,1,10,11)] MERGE_SUB_RAW <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Raw","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_RAW) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_RAW$GROUP <- "Raw" MERGE_SUB_TRIM <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Trim","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_TRIM) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_TRIM$GROUP <- "Trim" MERGE_SUB_HUMAN <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Human","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_HUMAN) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_HUMAN$GROUP <- "Human" MERGE_SUB_NEG <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Neg","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_NEG) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_NEG$GROUP <- "Neg" CAT_MERGE <- rbind(MERGE_SUB_RAW, MERGE_SUB_TRIM, MERGE_SUB_HUMAN, MERGE_SUB_NEG) CAT_MERGE_SUB <- CAT_MERGE[-which(CAT_MERGE$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","No")), ]
creator: Geoffrey D Hannigan
